rows.locks: Eligible source-owned C# is analyzed read-only into deterministic IR nodes, engine API touchpoints, behavioral-unit candidates, fidelity gaps, and Era R hand-off tasks without translating source.
Missing or unsupported source evidence is Yellow/Red with explicit gaps; a lossy import must never be graded clean/Green.
Every extracted unit starts oracle-missing and ported_claim_allowed=false, so ungated or auto-translated port claims fail the coverage invariant.
The deterministic digest/state-hash is stable for identical input order and changes when source behavior text changes, catching stale or nondeterministic analysis regressions.
Decompiler/ripped-source markers and missing source-only attestation are rejected and recorded Red; no decompiled code is copied into native behavior.
Coverage v90 records the one-way on-ramp, source-only clean-room legal boundary, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v90 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era R M108 legacy logic ingestion and behavioral-unit extraction. It locks source-project/open-text only analysis, one-way import to Ouroforge-native evidence/IR, clean-room re-derivation candidates, no decompiled-source copying, no Unity/Unreal/Godot runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy or degraded imports, deterministic digest/state-hash behavior, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
